name: Near-Threshold Coverage

tasks.title: Raise internal/pipeline from 92.7% to 94%+
tasks.body:
Pipeline needs ~1.3% more coverage. Key gaps by function:

- repository.go WriteBase (66.7%): MkdirAll and WriteFile error paths for writing base prompt files
- repository.go WriteAllBase (72.7%): iteration error over embedded templates, individual WriteBase failure mid-loop
- context_builder.go Build (72.2%): missing node state, missing spec files, template rendering failure, empty context fields
- class_repository.go Resolve (88.9%): class not found after reload, malformed class definition
- fragments.go ResolveAllFragments (90.9%): include list references a missing fragment (lines 79-81)
- fragments.go ResolvePromptTemplate (91.7%): invalid Go template syntax (lines 106-107)
- prompt.go AssemblePrompt (96.4%): skip assembly error, fragment resolution error (lines 21-38)
- repository.go Resolve (92.9%), ListFragments (91.7%): tier fallback edge cases

Focus on WriteBase/WriteAllBase (chmod error paths) and context_builder.Build (fixture-based tests) for the biggest gains. The fragment and template error paths are 1-2 lines each but collectively close the gap.

tasks.title: Raise internal/config from 93.3% to 94%+
tasks.body:
Config needs ~0.7% more coverage. Key gaps by function:

- repository.go Root (0.0%): simple getter that returns the root path. Trivially testable, just never called in existing tests
- repository.go WriteBase (66.7%): MkdirAll failure, writeFile error for base config tier
- repository.go writeTier (66.7%): directory creation failure, JSON marshal error (unlikely but guarded), file write error
- repository.go Load (78.3%): base tier file not found, custom tier parse error, local tier merge conflict, all three tiers missing
- config.go Load (84.2%): loadJSONFile returning malformed data, deep merge failure
- config.go structToMap (85.7%): marshal failure on known struct (defensive guard)

The Root() getter at 0% is trivially fixable. WriteBase and writeTier need chmod-based error tests. Load needs tests with intentionally malformed JSON in each tier.

tasks.title: Raise internal/state from 93.0% to 94%+
tasks.body:
State needs ~1% more coverage. Key gaps by function:

- store.go MutateNode (46.4%): this is the biggest single gap. Uncovered paths include lock acquisition failure, ReadNode error inside mutation callback, save error after successful mutation, concurrent mutation detection
- io.go atomicWriteJSON (68.0%): MkdirAll failure, CreateTemp failure, Write failure, Close failure, Rename failure (each is a distinct error path in the atomic write sequence)
- inbox_io.go InboxMutate (75.0%): load failure, callback error, save failure
- inbox_io.go InboxAppend (85.7%): load failure with new inbox creation, save error after append
- navigation.go parentInList (75.0%): parent not found, empty list, nested parent matching
- filelock_unix.go signalProcess (75.0%): signal send failure
- mutations.go TaskBlock (84.8%): blocking already-blocked task, blocking complete task (invalid transition)
- store.go ReadNode (88.9%), ReadIndex (83.3%), nodePath (85.7%): filesystem read errors, path construction edge cases
- propagation.go RecomputeState (87.1%): mixed task states producing unexpected aggregate

MutateNode at 46.4% is the priority target. It's the store's core write path and its uncovered branches represent real failure scenarios. atomicWriteJSON error paths need chmod tricks.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Near-Threshold Coverage is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify each of the three packages individually reaches 94%+ (not just their combined average)
Check that chmod-based tests include runtime.GOOS guards and t.Skip on Windows

breadcrumbs.text: Raised internal/pipeline from 92.6% to 98.0% by covering WriteBase/WriteAllBase write failures, Resolve template execution errors, ListFragments ResolveAll errors, Build per-task .md injection, ClassRepository non-ErrNotExist resolve paths, standalone fragment/template error paths, and AssemblePrompt failure modes.

Raised internal/config from 93.3% to 95.5% by covering Root() getter, WriteBase write error (via read-only base dir), writeTier MkdirAll and WriteFile failures (via read-only parent and local dirs), custom tier parse errors on both repository.Load and standalone Load, permission errors, and validation failures after successful tier merges.

Raised internal/state from 92.0% to 95.9% by covering MutateNode propagation paths (46→89%), ReadNode/ReadIndex corrupt-JSON errors, nodePath edge cases, MutateIndex corrupt-JSON, InboxMutate/InboxAppend error paths, parentInList no-dot, TaskBlock complete/already-blocked/auto-block-audit, RecomputeState blocked derivation, PropagateUp error+cycle, Propagate re-walk failure, and normalizeAuditState defaults.

Audit complete: verified all three coverage test files (pipeline, config, state). Build/vet/fmt clean, all tests pass with race detector. Coverage targets met: pipeline 98.0%, config 95.5%, state 95.9%. No correctness issues, no dead code, no duplication. Pre-existing daemon test failures confirmed unrelated to this node's work.

audit.result_summary: Raised three near-threshold internal packages above 90% coverage by adding targeted error-path tests: internal/pipeline from 92.6% to 98.0% (covering WriteBase/WriteAllBase write failures, Resolve template errors, ListFragments ResolveAll errors, Build per-task .md injection, ClassRepository non-ErrNotExist paths, standalone fragment/template errors, and AssemblePrompt failures); internal/config from 93.3% to 95.5% (covering Root() getter, WriteBase write errors, writeTier MkdirAll/WriteFile failures, custom tier parse errors, permission errors, and validation failures); internal/state from 92.0% to 95.9% (covering MutateNode propagation, ReadNode/ReadIndex corrupt JSON, nodePath edge cases, MutateIndex/InboxMutate/InboxAppend errors, TaskBlock edge cases, RecomputeState blocked derivation, PropagateUp error+cycle paths, and normalizeAuditState defaults).
